title: Package Design, Modules, Versions, Workspaces, and Dependency Provenance · Guided Systems Studio
summary: A team must split a command and library, publish a stable module, test a two-module workspace, and review dependency changes. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of a versioned multi-package module plan while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Organize commands, internal packages, public packages, names, imports, cycles, and documentation around cohesive APIs.
Interpret module, go, toolchain, require, replace, exclude, retract, and godebug directives without treating local overrides as releases.
Explain semantic import versioning, minimal version selection, module graphs, sums, proxies, and private-module boundaries.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

workshop case — A two-module workspace proposes dependency v3.2.0, a local replace, toolchain go1.26.5, and a public v2 package path. Explain MVS, checksum and proxy evidence, workspace-only success, API naming, and release compatibility.
